title: A 7-Year-Old Wore 50 Sweaters in a Library and Broke a World Record
script: Fifty sweaters — at once? On June 1, 7-year-old Skyler Rozell-Whitaker walked into the Monroeville Public Library in Pennsylvania with one goal: break the Guinness World Record for most sweaters worn simultaneously. The previous record was 46. He started layering. By sweater 40, he was roughly marshmallow-shaped. At sweater 46, the crowd started chanting his name. At sweater 47, he officially broke the record. He kept going to 50, just to guarantee it. Guinness reviewed the footage, confirmed the count, and issued the certificate. All 50 sweaters were donated items — they go to the library's fall sweater drive. Skyler is 7 years old and now holds a world record nobody at his school can beat. Could you outlast Skyler at sweater 46?
